sentences.text: Right?
Yay!
Hello!
Thank you.
Good, how are you?
It's Friday, it's a long weekend, it's about to be a long weekend, so I'm good!
Yeah, you're all ready for the… for 5 o'clock.
I'm ready for it, I'm feeling it!
Beta Friday!
You have any fun plans for the weekend?
Not yet, I'm sure they'll evolve at some point.
Yeah.
I know, I'm always kind of planning, because I have the young kids or whatnot, so what day's a beach day, what day's a cookout, what day's a birthday party?
Right, right.
Although I am escaping for Girls Day tomorrow for a little bit, so…
Well, thanks for taking a few minutes to hop on and booking some time with me.
I'm assuming you saw my email.
Did you use my link to book this?
I think so.
Okay, cool.
Anything specific that, like, I had mentioned in the email that kind of prompted you to be open to listening, or…?
No, I think one of… somebody from your company kept calling and blowing up our phones, so we were just trying to, you know, just circle back.
Fair, fair.
Yeah, Josh, he's a… he's a persistent one, but here we are anyway, so… I appreciate it.
Well, so really, I mean, so we were reaching out, so… I don't… are you familiar with Maven?
Do you… like, do you… are you….
I'm familiar with Rentable, and to be honest, I think most of us collectively have not been a fan of Rentable, but, hopefully your product is better than the rest.
Fair enough.
Is your experience with our ILS, with the ILS side of things, the rentable ILS?
Yes,
Okay, that makes sense.

So, Rentiful's, like, our parent company, and I know we obviously have our ILS, services, but I handle all things Maven-related, so, our Google product, our social product, reputation, and listings management all fall under the Maven umbrella.
Rentable has, like, their ILS, then there's Maven, and then there's ApartmentIQ, which is our market survey.

So we kind of all… we act almost as, like, 3 totally separate sales forces, and almost totally separate companies, for that matter, so… I hate to hear that your experience with Rentable hasn't been great, but Maven is… is… completely focused on different, different goals and a completely different suite of products.
So, hopefully that helps a little bit if… did you just not have a good experience with, like, the… like, the leads in the list… in the listing side of things, or…?
Yeah, that and, … it seems like you're, you know, your salespeople are very good at talking, let's say that.
So, it just gets a bit exhausting saying no constantly, so….
Fair enough, fair enough I know that Josh had also talked to Joshua, from your company, and he had mentioned that you had been exploring and somewhat close to, utilizing some of Sochi solutions.

So, I guess, for the sake of today, I would love to run you through the, … the, you know, the product suite that we offer, but I would also just like to understand, kind of like, what are your initiatives or things that you're focused on that's bringing you to Sochi?
Like, is there something specific with them that you are looking for?
… Just to add.
So….
Yeah, so we were with Apartments.
com using just their, review, monitoring, and, social media content.
Okay.
So, I'm relatively new to Lind, but prior to me getting here, Justin and Josue had explored other companies to see what other solutions were out there.
The decision was made to go with reputation.
com, for surveys, social media monitoring, the social calendar, and… business listings.

So we started that relationship, back May-ish, two months in, it has been a colossal failure, of epic proportions, and so, we decided to both go our separate ways, at which point we started looking for an alternate solution, prior to being with Lind, I was with Embry, and we used Sochi, so that was where I, you know, first initially reached out.
We went ahead and did a demo with Satisfaction and with Jay Turner also, just to verify different products, determined that Sochi of the three, was the best.
did not know your product even existed.
We're about to sign with Sochi, so just in the, you know, effort of Solid due diligence, we set up your demo.
Got it.
That's helpful.
Okay, so Apartments.
com for reviews and social, your social calendar, and then also reputation, and that's what was the….
Yeah, we left Departments.

com to go to Reputation because… Because it had a full suite of all four things, because previously they didn't have surveys, and they didn't have, the business listing piece, so they went with reputation because it offered the full suite, and then, like I said, the whole process, everything about it on every level was just atrocious, and so, so we… parted ways, and, and so, you know, like I said, now we're looking for a solution, that would have to be implemented very, very quickly, given that, you know, we're at this crossroads with reputation.
Got it.
Okay, that's… that's really helpful to know.
How many properties are in the LIND portfolio?
Over 80, but we would not be enrolling all of them in something like this, since some of them are affordable.
Okay, what's roughly, like, the number that you… that there is a fit there?
Okay.
And then… it sounds like reputation is a big focus, and then also you were doing social media content, so is that, like, are you looking for posting and those types of services?
No, it was just a content calendar, … they weren't actually creating the content for us.
Okay.
So… so yeah, so it was survey, you know, surveys, review monitoring, the social content calendar, and then business listings.
Okay, and then with Sochi, are you exploring just, like, pretty much the same package, but just with them as opposed to reputation?
Yes.
View surveys, calendar, okay, okay, listings, business listings, okay.
Do you do anything or focus at all on the Google business profiles as well?
Yeah, that's through the business listing piece.
Okay.
Just the business listing, but any, like, content, or Q&A, or anything of that nature?
The Q&A, I think, is handled through their business listing section, and then the content itself is pushed out through the social calendar.
commercial calendar.
So, yeah, it's pushing out to Google, Facebook, and Instagram, and TikTok simultaneously.
Yep, got it.
Okay, yep, that makes perfect sense.
And then, from a, …
what was I gonna say?
Oh, from a content creation standpoint, like, from the calendar, do you have, like, your on-site teams doing that, or who's creating the content, or who will be creating the content?
… That will be up to the teams themselves.
If they want to do it themselves, they're perfectly, welcome to, but we have an in-house agency.
Okay.
So our… our in-house team, if the sites want to budget for that, then… then we can take that on for them.
Got it.
Okay, so the in-house agency can't… do it, if the site's budget for it.
Okay.
So our… our bundle, I guess, that I referenced kind of covers an array of different things.
So, it sounds like, reviews and listings management is at the forefront of… of your mind, and then also some of that social piece with the calendar.
And then from a Google business standpoint, that ties into your listing.
So… We kind of encompass all of them, we can separate them out, we can bundle them together, but so, we, in full transparency, for reputation and listings management, we white-label BirdEye.
Have you explored them at all?
No.

Okay, so our reputation, our listings management product is BirdEye, and then we in-house built a automated social posting, slash the ability to post as well through our backend, and then a Google Business optimization piece, where we push the content out to Google Business.
So very, like, you're hitting those pieces that you're getting with Sochi.

The biggest difference, like, we've… sometimes we work simultaneously with Sochi, and other times where we can be a full replacement for Sochi, so… When it comes to the Google business profile, that's probably where you're gonna see the biggest difference with us in Sochi, so… Our social and our Google product is built in-house, and specifically that Google product is really heavily focused on the optimization of your Google profiles in more ways than necessarily just, like, standard content.
So… what I mean by that is the frequency of our content, the relevancy of our content.
So I can show you, kind of, a run-through of, kind of, all of it, if that makes sense, and then I'll give you some pricing, and we can certainly go from there.
But does that make sense to you?
Any questions on anything, at least so far?
No, that sounds fine.
Okay, cool.

I'll start with the Google piece, … And so, that way you kind of have an idea of the products that we, you know, build in-house, and then I'll show you the listings management and reputation side of things as well, but… From a Google Business perspective, so our tool is a fully automated solution to optimize the profiles.
But the key factor in it is it's completely automated, Christy.
So, the idea behind this is you have 45 properties, right?
Like, to be able to manually update all of those Google business profiles beyond just, like, the listings management side, like the NAPAC side of things, is just a super heavy lift.
So, a majority of our partners use us because our tools are very automated, and the way that we do that is through our syndication.
with, through our integrations with your property management software.
So, who does Lind use for their property management software?
… I'm sorry, I was….
No, no, you're fine.
Like, if you were sending an ILS feed to somewhere, like, is that coming through?
Do you have Rent Cafe, or…?
Yeah, no real pains.
You have RealPage.
Okay, perfect.
So, we would integrate with your RealPage and pull through your syndication feed, and that's really what allows us to be able to automate the social content and the Google content.
So obviously, you know.
Google is such an important part of your properties, right?
Like, Google dominates search, and your Google business profiles, Google tells us this, is the number one way for your properties to be found on Google.

And through relevancy and frequency and uniqueness of updates, Google rewards on that, and so over time, you really build up your organic by doing these types of updates in conjunction with things like your nap pack, your name, address, your phone, the things that you're talking about from a listing side of things.
So… Essentially, we do this in a couple different ways.
So we do it with automated posting, including concession posts, a live pricing and availability feed to show what you have available in real time, at a floor plan level.
We can also pull through your amenities as well to display your amenities, if you have some properties with nice amenities that you want to be highlighted.
And then also with updating your Q&A.
So I know that Sochi does some things with Q&A.
Our Q&A is a little different, where we're updating the answers to your Q&A every single day, so we place the question and the answer.
So I'll show you each of these features a little bit more.
I'll show you a live property.
I think a live property is… this is a Willowbridge property.
So, just to get into the posting piece, so, I know you had mentioned that Sochi will… push the content out to your social pages and to your Google pages.
So, where we're really gonna differ here is this is fully automated, and you get posts every single day.
What we know and why we do this is Google.
Google says we want to see frequent, relevant, property-specific content.
That's what's going to impact your organic most over time.
So, every single day, we post a unique piece of content with a photo that matches that content.
call to action to learn more about your property.

We also post your concessions if you run them, and if you want them on the profile once a week as well, and our AI is smart enough to pull these off when they're no longer valid, so you never have any outdated concessions living on the profile.
You can incorporate your SEO keywords and phrases into this content if you wanted to, any brand language guidelines that you might have.
So initially, we pull through every… pull through everything, and we can automate content right away.
If there was any properties that you said, hey, Lisa, I really want to focus in on these 5 keywords or these 5 phrases, we would just add that into the backend.

But totally automated, nothing for you to review, create, and then a call to action to learn more, which you can put We can put tracking codes on for you if you want to see the clicks that are coming through from the content.
Any questions about that?
Nope.
And then… our live pricing and availability feed.
So this is a really nice way for prospects to see exactly what you have available in real time, right on the Google profile.
And then we give a nice description of that.
We put in your amenities, so another place where we're calling out your amenities, Google likes that, and then a call to action, again, to visit your website.
So, we get a lot of feedback from our customers that this helps drive really qualified traffic to the website.

They know what you have available, they can see that starting price point, and so when they get to the website, you know, they're kind of… A little further along in the process if they're clicking through.
Any questions there?
Nope.

I'm zipping through a little bit, because I know I'm going to cover kind of a lot with you if we're looking at all the full package here, so by all means, please tell me if there's anything that I'm going too fast on.
And then Q&A is the other piece.
So we place the question, and then update the answer every single day.
It's about 10 to 15 questions, and we basically got these questions, Christy, from knowing what are the most frequently asked questions.
So we did a big data poll, and it shows that Most commonly, people are asking about your availability and some specific amenities, like your fitness center, your pet policies, gyms.
We highlight EV charging stations.
Your rent specials, we'll pull them in here, your office hours, so you'll see, like, these questions were placed 7 months ago, but the hours are up, the answers are updated every single day.
So this'll update again today, it'll say as of the 29th, yes, we have a studio available.

So another place where we're highlighting your availability, your amenities, this is really impactful from a GEO standpoint, the way that these Questions are pushed through, and the formatting, comes up on search as well that way, so… big Google play here from an SEO standpoint, but also, again, making sure that your profiles are fully optimized, robust, and this is all pulling from your syndication feed as your source of truth.
So, no manual work on your end.

And we could also get the, … this would be one where I can show you, we can also highlight your amenities, so if you wanted to, for example, a game room, it'll give you a little description of your amenities, so you can opt into that as well.
So that's the… that's the Google side of things.

Really, like I said, the combination of all of these automated features over time builds up that organic SEO presence, and then saves you a ton of time trying to optimize these profiles from a more manual standpoint.

I think when you're thinking about this compared to Sochi, obviously the Q&A is a bit different, your live pricing and availability is different, and I think the biggest thing is property-specific, relevant content daily to Google.
not content that's kind of cross-posted around, which often, you know, is not the ideal way when it comes to content for Google.
We just know Google likes to see their own content.
Google's world, and so we're… we're living in it.
So that's… that's kind of the big things I would… I would enforce there, or… or kind of explain there, is… and then just the syndication, so being able to be integrated makes this totally automated.
And then we just had so many people that were like, hey, we love the content you're putting out on Google, can you put something out like that on social?
So, our social tool, even though the content is not the same, it's automated the same way with the information that we're pulling from your feed.
We post twice a week automatically for you.
So, this is property-specific content, holiday-style posts, scheduling a tour, templates, lifestyle templates.
Some cute pet templates, things of that nature, so it's very social-style content.
It's different than what's going out to your Google, so we won't cross-post that content.

And then what I will say about, about this content, too, is it's… Google has just recently announced that they're gonna be indexing social posts, so because they are property-specific, these will be helpful in that fashion as well, when you're thinking about your organic.
Any questions about the social posting, the Google?
Nope.
That's good.
Am I zipping?
Am I going too fast?
No, you're fine.
Okay.
I'll show you the dashboard.
So, we have a dashboard that you can log into.
It'll show you all of your properties.
You can select the property, or you can select the channels that you want to see.
So, for example, if you just wanted to see your Google posts that are going out for maybe a single property, whatever it may be.

You can see a rolling 30 days of content that's scheduled to go out for all of the channels, so if you want to see your Facebook and your Instagram, you can see your Facebook and your Instagram, so it's indicated by the, this property only has Facebook, but indicated by this little icon here, you can edit or re… regenerate content, so say you saw something you didn't like, you can edit it.
Or you can regenerate it, and it'll just create a new piece of content.

I think the biggest thing I always like to tell people when I'm showing them this is we are automation-forward, meaning that you never have to log into this backend if you didn't want to, and your content will still go out as it's planned to, but we do have People who want visibility into the content that's going out, and so we can… we can kind of give you the best of both worlds with the automation, but also give you the visibility.
Okay.
post from back here.

So, if you do want your on-site teams to have access to posts, Christy, we can give them just their property, or a grouping of properties, if there's a regional, where they can go in and they can put out additional content in addition to that twice a week.
So, they can select a group of properties, you can bulk post, you can schedule, select your source, and then, you know, you can upload an image in your content and have it go out for you.
And then we could do hashtags in your post as well for social, and then we can also incorporate any of your brand language or SEO keywords and phrases into the social content as well.
So all this content is, I would say, you know.

To some degree, customizable, just to make sure that we're really hitting on the things that are, like, most important to you when it comes to your brand voice and the things that you want these posts to be focused in on.
Any questions there?
Nope.
Why am I making this so easy on me?
And then I will show you our reputation dash.
So that's Google and social.
Any thoughts, feedback?
Do they wait?
No.
Can you hear me, Christy?
Yeah, what was the question?
I was just curious, any thoughts or feedback on what you've seen so far?
I mean, it's interesting, so….
… So this is our reputation dash.
So… By taking a look at this, essentially you're seeing on the first page here, you're seeing… you could set this up however you want with whatever sites.
You're seeing all the properties and their overall score.
You could filter this however you'd like, if you want to filter it by specific properties or a group of properties.
And really the nice thing with this is you can make it as automated or not automated as you want.

We have partners like CAF, CAF, that have all automated responses, and then we have other partners who do 4- and 5-star responses on auto-response, and then they want to go in and they want to respond to reviews that are lesser than 4 stars.
for Socier, for what you've done in the past, how are… how are they responding?
Are you responding?
Are they responding?
Like, what's that process look like?
We've had a mixture of… of… Of both, so I'm not sure what we'll do going forward.
Okay.
So is it just AI-based, or is there humans responding?
So, we don't specifically respond.
We have a couple different options for responses.

So, we can set, we have, like, for example, like I said, CAF, they have tons of different templates that round robin according to what the review is, so that way you're not getting, like, very generic responses.
They've made lots of templates, so we can set up templates for you.
And then we can set it so it auto-replies if, say, it's a 4- and 5-star review.
Now, say you get a less review, and you want to go in and you want to handle those.
You can do replies a couple different ways.
You can go in and you can manually reply by just typing in and replying.

You can use this AI button, and it will create an AI-generated reply that you can then edit or change the tone of if you wanted, make it shorter, make it longer, or you can just manually, like, you can just manually go in and create your own reply.
So, it's kind of like I said, this is, like, the type of thing, when we set it up, we say, like, hey, Christy, like, how do you want this to look?
How automated do you want it to be?
How many templates do you want us to create for you?
You know, we can set this up for you, kind of however you want it set up.
And then… You can create QR codes in here.

We do some really cool stuff with, like, AI, if you wanted to do, like, their AI summaries, giving you, like, hey, this is a sentiment of the last 100 reviews, and it'll create kind of some bullet points for you.

Some of our partners will use this, where maybe they'll say, hey, I want to pull an AI summary for this regional's 10 properties, and then they'll send that and say, hey, these are the things that we're seeing in your reviews, and they use that as a coaching tool, so this is a really popular feature.
Again, you can create QR codes in here, so if you wanted to have, like, QR codes for a resident event, you can create a QR code by property.
But the idea is to make this a really easy, all-in-one place for you to reply to the reviews using templates, using AI, or using, you know, just doing it manually.
And then we have the ability to run campaigns, so we can set these campaigns up for you.

Where we would be able to set triggers, so if you want to send out, send out a campaign for maybe a completed tour or a maintenance request, whatever it may be, once it's completed, it will shoot out a campaign via text and email, one or the other, or both.
Where it'll ask them to leave a review or contact directly if it wasn't a good experience.

So the idea is to try to get away from those negative reviews, but still, garner the positive ones, but you have to give them, obviously, that ability, no matter what, to write a review, whether it's good or bad.
This all integrates with your ReelPage, so you… we have all the contact information's all set up in there for you, so there's no manual lift here.
And then we can run surveys, we have reporting, an array of reporting in here, and then from a listings management perspective.

Most of those tools look somewhat the same, it's not exactly the… Most exciting of tools, but would show you all of your, … all of your… properties, you can set who you're set up with, and then, essentially, we can update… there's, like, 60 different directories with… with the map pack, so… the map pack there, so…
Like, for example, this one's showing all… these are all the ones that we're connected to.
Any questions?
I… and again, this is kind of a high level of this, but just curious, questions, initial feedback?
I don't have any questions.
I've got Shane and Justin in here too, y'all.
Hi, Shane.
No, I don't necessarily have anything, it's just, is there a… is there a survey aspect to y'all's product?
Yep, we can run surveys through our listing, or through our reputation as well.

If you wanted to run surveys, reporting, you have a customer success manager that's, you know, typically we meet monthly when we have our partners on these, these, you know, the bundle package with all these different services together.
But yeah, so we can run surveys, automated campaigns, responses, and upon implementation, we will set everything up for you.
It sounds like you've used Listings Management Tool in the past.
I don't know if you have a spreadsheet or anything that you've used in the past.
Initially, we can do initial setup of all your listings for you.
And then again, you will have a dedicated customer success manager who would meet with you, really as frequently as you want.
Typically, we meet monthly, but if that's too much or too little, we can meet as frequently as you'd like.
And then again, you get, you know, your listings, your reputation, plus you get that true optimization of the Google profile.
true automation when it comes to the content for Google and for your social pages.
Awesome, thank you.
And how does your pricing work?
So, you… so I can price this a couple different ways for you.
So, I guess, with what you're looking at and what you're seeing, just, like, from a feedback perspective, is this something where you would want to bundle all the services together in one?
Yes.
Okay, perfect.
So, if we were gonna do that, we would be able to offer you, the Google, the social, the reputation, the listings for $279 a month of property.
Okay.

So that's getting you all of the features on Google, the automated social posting, plus the ability to post content as much as you'd want in the back end, plus reputation management with your surveys, with your campaigns, all integrated, and your listings management as well.
Okay.
Is that, on par, or palatable, or similar to what you… what you're looking at with Sochi?
… It's… it's a little higher, we're still waiting on pricing back from Satisfaction and Jay Turner, so, I think until we get, you know, pricing from all four, we'll… Decide at that point.
… Yeah, it's… I mean, it's… the AI stuff is, you know, interesting.
I'm curious, like, how much, even after just, like, our initial conversation here, like, is the Google, like.
on your radar.
So, to be totally honest with all of you, like, the Google is our primary, like, focus.
We build that product, same thing with the social, like, those are the, like.

that product is our baby, and we've grown tremendously with that product specifically, because the Google profiles have just continued to get more and more important, and so optimizing them has continued to get more important.

And… So she somewhat touches it, but not to the level that we do, and then, like, Jay Turner, for example, in Satisfaction is a totally, you know, totally different realm, more focused on that listings and reputation side and surveys, right?

So, is that something that's interesting to you enough that that… that you see the value in, even if it was a little bit more, or even if we… we often will price match or beat pricing, you know, when it comes to Sochi, for example.
We go up against Sochi Most frequently.
Not typically Satisfaction, Jay Turner, to be honest with you.

I mean, we have gone up against them for reputation and listings, but… due to the nature of our product being more focused with the Google and social, and then being able to add reputation and listings management in, it's not as common that we go up against them, but… Is that Google piece important to you as well?
I mean, it has some importance, but I don't know that it's the end-all be-all.
Sure.
And now… right now, Christy, do you know, like, are you posting on the profiles at all, or is that… there's no… currently, there's no content going out on them?
On social media?
On Google, on your Google profiles.
Oh, on Google, no, I don't believe there is content going out currently, since we're kind of in between providers.
Sure, that makes sense.
Okay.
Well, I… I can send everything over to you, a breakdown of the pricing, what would be included in the package.
For 45 properties, we can have you live.
It sounds like you're really looking to get a solution locked in pretty quickly and need to get things live.
We would have you live I would say… 10, 14 business days or less, especially if it's something where, you know, you're like, hey, we need to get going on this, like, right away.
… But yeah, from, like, a next steps perspective, I can send everything over to you.
I know that you said you're waiting on some pricing for… from the others.
Would it make sense for us to touch base again in a week, or how do you want me to follow up with you, and where should we go?
Yeah, if you wanted to send me everything, and then I can circle back with our boss and see what his thoughts are, and then we can figure out next steps.
Do you have any questions, or concerns, or…?
I don't, guys, you… Nope, I'm good.
Appreciate it.

Hopefully that was… I know I threw a lot at you at once, because it's, you know, it's a lot of different, products combined, but if anything does come up, or if you do have any questions, by all means, please reach out.
I'm happy to answer anything for you.
Sounds great, thank you so much.
Thanks for your time.
Have a wonderful long weekend, everybody.
